policies.name: Universal Pre-K (Ages 3-4)
policies.description: Federally funded universal pre-K based on Perry Preschool and European models.
policies.rationale: Perry Preschool RCT: 40-year follow-up shows $7-12 ROI per dollar. France, Denmark, Finland all have universal pre-K with better educational outcomes.
policies.currentStatus: Only 34% of US 3-year-olds enrolled; varies wildly by state
policies.recommendedTarget: Federal funding for universal enrollment by age 3

policies.name: Pragmatic Clinical Trial Funding Reform
policies.description: Redirect NIH funding from basic research overhead to pragmatic real-world clinical trials with mandatory data sharing.
policies.rationale: NIH spends $48B/yr but 70%+ goes to indirect costs and basic research with low translation rates. UK NIHR model: pragmatic trials embedded in NHS produce actionable evidence at 1/10th the cost. PCORIs pragmatic trials show 3x faster clinical adoption.
policies.currentStatus: NIH: $48B/yr, <10% on pragmatic trials, 85% of findings fail to replicate
policies.recommendedTarget: Mandate 30%+ of NIH budget for pragmatic trials with open data requirements

policies.name: National Paid Family Leave (12 weeks)
policies.description: 12 weeks paid family leave at 66% wage replacement. US is the only OECD country without it.
policies.rationale: Every other OECD country has paid leave. California's program: 18% reduction in nursing home use by new parents, higher breastfeeding rates, no negative employment effects.
policies.currentStatus: US is only OECD country with 0 weeks mandated paid family leave
policies.recommendedTarget: 12 weeks at 66% wage replacement, funded by 0.4% payroll tax

policies.name: Military Spending Reduction to OECD Floor
policies.description: Reduce military spending from $2,052/cap to OECD efficient floor of $279/cap (7.4x overspend). OECD data: US ranks 23/23 in military spending efficiency. Ireland achieves life expectancy 82 at $226/cap. Redirect $601B/yr savings to Universal Dividend.
policies.rationale: OECD efficiency analysis: US ranks dead last (23/23) in military spending efficiency. Ireland spends $226/cap with life expectancy 82; Spain $331/cap with 82.87; Austria $339/cap with 81.3. US spends $2,052/cap with life expectancy 76.93. Military spending has R²=0.006 correlation with life expectancy — zero relationship. $601B/yr savings → $4,519/household/yr as Universal Dividend.
policies.currentStatus: US spends $886B/yr (7.4x OECD efficient floor), ranks 23/23 in efficiency
policies.recommendedTarget: Phased reduction to $279/cap ($95B total). $601B/yr savings → Universal Dividend Pool

policies.name: Earned Income Tax Credit Expansion
policies.description: Expand EITC for childless workers and increase phase-out thresholds.
policies.rationale: EITC lifts ~6M people out of poverty annually. Research shows improved infant health, better school performance, and increased labor force participation. Bipartisan support historically.
policies.currentStatus: $63B annual cost; benefits phase out at $59K for families
policies.recommendedTarget: Double benefit for childless workers ($1,500→$3,000); raise income cap

policies.name: Shift Drug Policy from Criminal to Health Approach
policies.description: Decriminalize personal drug use, fund treatment and harm reduction. Based on Portugal model (2001).
policies.rationale: Portugal decriminalized in 2001: drug deaths dropped 80%, HIV among users dropped 90%, treatment uptake tripled. Czech Republic, Switzerland, and Netherlands show similar results.
policies.currentStatus: US spends $40B/yr on drug enforcement; 1.5M arrests annually
policies.recommendedTarget: Decriminalize personal use, redirect enforcement budget to treatment

policies.name: Federal Incentives for Zoning Reform
policies.description: Federal grants conditioned on local upzoning, elimination of single-family-only zoning.
policies.rationale: Tokyo has no housing crisis because they allow building. Minneapolis eliminated single-family zoning: rents stabilized. Oregon statewide upzoning reduced housing cost growth. Restrictive zoning costs the US economy $1.6T/yr in lost GDP.
policies.currentStatus: 75% of US residential land zoned single-family only
policies.recommendedTarget: Condition federal transportation/HUD grants on local zoning reform

policies.name: Occupational Licensing Reform
policies.description: Reduce unnecessary licensing requirements that affect 25% of US workers.
policies.rationale: Licensed occupations grew from 5% to 25% of workforce since 1950s. States with less licensing have no difference in service quality but 10-15% lower consumer prices. Interior designers need a license in some states; paramedics don't in others.
policies.currentStatus: 25% of US workers need government license; varies wildly by state
policies.recommendedTarget: Federal mutual recognition framework; sunset reviews for all licenses

policies.name: Permanent Expanded Child Tax Credit
policies.description: Make the 2021 expanded CTC ($3,600/child) permanent and fully refundable.
policies.rationale: 2021 expanded CTC cut child poverty by 46% in 6 months — largest single-year reduction ever. Canada's similar program reduced child poverty by 30%. Cost: ~$100B/yr.
policies.currentStatus: Reverted to $2,000/child, not fully refundable (excludes poorest families)

policies.name: Carbon Fee-and-Dividend
policies.description: Tax carbon emissions, return 100% of revenue as equal citizen dividend. Based on British Columbia model.
policies.rationale: British Columbia: emissions down 5-15% vs control, GDP growth maintained. Switzerland carbon tax reduced emissions 20%. Dividend protects low-income households.
policies.currentStatus: No federal carbon price; 12 states have cap-and-trade or carbon tax
policies.recommendedTarget: $50/ton starting price, rising $10/yr, 100% returned as dividend

policies.name: Singapore-Style Healthcare (Universal + Competition)
policies.description: Mandatory health savings + catastrophic insurance + price transparency. Singapore spends 4% GDP with better outcomes than US at 17%. OECD data: US ranks 23/23 in health spending efficiency at $10,333/cap vs floor of $3,142/cap (3.3x overspend). Savings: $2.4T/yr.
policies.rationale: Singapore: life expectancy 84 (US: 77), infant mortality 1.7 (US: 5.4), healthcare spending 4% GDP (US: 17%). OECD efficiency analysis: US ranks dead last (23/23) at $10,333/cap. Portugal achieves life expectancy 81.4 at $2,924/cap. Floor spending: $3,142/cap. Potential savings: $2.4T/yr → $18,329/household/yr as dividend.
policies.currentStatus: US spends $4.3T/yr (17% GDP), ranks 23/23 in OECD health efficiency
policies.recommendedTarget: Singapore 3M model: mandatory HSAs + catastrophic insurance + price transparency. Target: $3,142/cap (OECD floor)

policies.name: NEPA & Permitting Reform
policies.description: Streamline environmental review timelines from 4.5 years to 2 years for infrastructure projects.
policies.rationale: Average NEPA review takes 4.5 years. Canada and EU complete equivalent reviews in 2 years. Delays cost $3.7T in deferred infrastructure investment.
policies.currentStatus: Average environmental review: 4.5 years; 40,000+ pages of regulation
policies.recommendedTarget: 2-year maximum review with categorical exclusions for clean energy

note: Generated using Bradford Hill scoring and welfare calculation from real cross-country evidence.
